data.title: Bond Market Adjusts Expectations Amid Iran Conflict
data.description: Bond investors are facing a shift in their expectations for monetary policy due to the ongoing conflict in Iran. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting how the situation is impacting financial markets. The anticipation of a more lenient monetary policy this year is being challenged as geopolitical tensions rise, affecting investor sentiment and market dynamics. The conflict has introduced uncertainty, prompting investors to reassess their strategies in light of potential economic implications. As the situation develops, market participants are closely monitoring the impact on global financial conditions.

data.title: Gemini Cuts 30% of Staff as Losses Widen to $585M in 2025
data.description: Gemini reduced its workforce by 30%, bringing total headcount down to roughly 445 employees since January. The company posted a $585M full-year loss while Q4 revenue beat analyst expectations at $60.3M, according to BeInCrypto.

data.title: Polymarket Sees $219,000 Bet Against U.S. Alien Confirmation by 2027
data.description: A newly established cryptocurrency address has placed a significant wager of approximately $219,000 on Polymarket, betting against the U.S. confirming the existence of aliens before 2027. According to NS3.AI, this address also made a smaller bet in a separate Polymarket market, wagering against the confirmation of alien existence by March 31st. These bets reflect skepticism about imminent official announcements regarding extraterrestrial life.

data.title: DeFiance Capital CEO Predicts Continued Pressure on Iran Amid Middle East Tensions
data.description: DeFiance Capital CEO Arthur has expressed concerns over the ongoing tensions in the Middle East, suggesting that a short-term cooling is unlikely. According to NS3.AI, Arthur believes that the United States and Israel may continue to exert pressure on Iran, potentially leading to further disruptions in global supply chains in the coming weeks.

data.title: After approximately 14 years of inactivity, a whale transferred out 0.00079 BTC, leaving it with a floating profit of over $148 million on its holdings of approximately 2,100 BTC.
data.description: According to Lookonchain monitoring, address 1NB3ZX, after approximately 14 years of inactivity, made a transaction, transferring out 0.00079 BTC, worth about $56. This address received 2100 BTC on July 5, 2012, at a price of approximately $6.59. Its current holdings are worth approximately $148 million, representing a return of approximately 10,710 times.

data.title: Taiwan to Miss WTO Conference Over Delegation Labeling Dispute
data.description: Taiwan announced it will not attend a World Trade Organization (WTO) conference in Cameroon due to a disagreement over the labeling of its delegation in visa documents. Bloomberg posted on X that the issue arose when Cameroon's hosts referred to Taiwan's delegation in a manner that Taiwan found unacceptable. This decision underscores ongoing tensions related to Taiwan's international representation and the sensitivity surrounding its diplomatic status. Taiwan's absence from the conference highlights the challenges it faces in participating in global forums where its status is contested.

data.title: BlackRock deposited approximately 47,700 ETH and 544 BTC into Coinbase Prime.
data.description: According to Lookonchain, BlackRock addresses deposited 47,728 ETH, worth approximately $102 million, and 544 BTC, worth approximately $38.3 million, into Coinbase Prime.

data.title: TAO Surges Over 20% Amid Nvidia CEO's Remarks on Bittensor
data.description: TAO experienced a significant increase of over 20% in its value, currently priced at $304.1, according to Odaily. This surge follows comments made by Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang during the All-In podcast, where he discussed Bittensor's achievements. Huang highlighted the successful training of a 4 billion parameter Llama model on Subnet 3, accomplished through collaborative distributed computing, describing it as a remarkable technological feat.

data.title: Mexican Retailer Reduces Workforce in Fintech Division
data.description: One of Mexico's largest retailers is implementing layoffs within its fintech startup as it scales back its ambitions. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the company's decision to reduce its workforce in response to changing market conditions. The retailer, known for its significant presence in the Mexican market, is reassessing its strategy in the fintech sector amid economic challenges. This move reflects a broader trend among businesses to adapt to evolving financial landscapes and prioritize sustainable growth. The company aims to streamline operations and focus on core areas to enhance efficiency and competitiveness. As the fintech industry continues to evolve, firms are increasingly evaluating their positions and making strategic adjustments to align with market demands.

data.title: Ukraine Deploys Experts to Gulf Region to Combat Iranian Drones
data.description: Ukraine has dispatched over 200 experts to the Gulf region to assist in countering Iranian drone threats. According to Jin10, this move is part of Ukraine's broader strategy to enhance regional security and collaborate with Gulf nations in addressing the challenges posed by Iranian drone technology. The deployment underscores the growing concern over the use of drones in regional conflicts and the need for international cooperation to mitigate these threats.

data.title: The new address withdrew approximately 139,600 HYPE tokens from the Gate, worth approximately $5.49 million.
data.description: According to Onchain Lens monitoring, a newly created address withdrew 139,557 HYPE tokens from Gate, worth approximately $5.49 million.

data.title: DeFiance CEO: The situation in the Middle East is unlikely to de-escalate in the short term; the US and Israel may continue to pressure Iran.
data.description: DeFiance Capital CEO Arthur stated in an article on the X platform that a "TACO" (Trump's last-minute retreat) and a de-escalation of the Middle East situation are unlikely in the short term, as the US and Israel are currently exerting continuous pressure on Iran. He pointed out that global supply chains may be further disrupted in the coming weeks. According to a previous report by Axios, the Trump administration is considering occupying or blocking Iran's Kharg Island to force it to reopen the Strait of Hormuz.

data.title: VIX Index Rises Amid Market Concerns
data.description: The VIX Index, a key measure of market volatility, has increased by 1.38 points, reaching 25.44. According to ChainCatcher, this rise indicates growing concerns among investors about potential market fluctuations.

data.title: Global Stock Market Confidence Wanes Amid Middle East Tensions
data.description: Investor confidence in global stock markets is beginning to falter as tensions in the Middle East escalate. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting that while investors initially maintained composure, the ongoing conflict is now causing concern. The situation has led to increased market volatility, with investors closely monitoring developments in the region. Analysts suggest that the uncertainty may impact investment strategies and market stability in the coming weeks. The geopolitical tensions are adding pressure to an already fragile global economic environment, prompting investors to reassess their positions.

data.title: UK Expands Vaccination Program Amid Rising Meningococcal Cases
data.description:
The number of invasive meningococcal infections in the United Kingdom has increased to at least 27 cases, prompting authorities to expand the vaccination program. According to RTHK, the decision comes as a precautionary measure to curb the spread of the disease.

In Hong Kong, the Chairman of the Scientific Committee on Vaccine Preventable Diseases, Lau Yu-lung, stated that the risk of transmission in the region remains low. However, he advised students planning to study in the UK to ensure they receive both types of vaccines as a preventive measure.

data.title: Crypto Investors in UK Emerge as Political Force Amid Growing Influence
data.description: Industry leaders have highlighted the increasing political influence of cryptocurrency holders in the UK, as millions of users now possess digital assets. According to NS3.AI, this demographic is becoming a significant political bloc that parties can no longer overlook. The article also draws attention to the US election cycle, where crypto executives and investors contributed over $130 million. A survey revealed that 64% of digital asset investors consider a candidate's stance on cryptocurrency as very important.

data.title: CertiK joins the UN anti-fraud cooperation framework to help combat cross-border Web3-related crimes.
data.description: On March 20, CertiK, the world's largest Web3 security company, announced that it had submitted an anti-fraud commitment at the 2026 UN Global Fraud Summit. The commitment will support the global fight against cross-border Web3-related crimes through technical support, threat intelligence, and investigative collaboration. The summit, co-hosted by the United Nations Office on Drugs and Crime (UNODC) and Interpol, saw commitments from tech and financial giants such as Google and Lloyds Banking Group. According to the disclosure, CertiK's commitment focuses on three main areas: first, leveraging its security platform to provide real-time threat intelligence and on-chain risk monitoring, enhancing the ability of regulatory and law enforcement agencies to identify and respond to anomalous activities; second, assembling an expert team to provide technical support and forensic assistance for major cross-border attacks; and third, regularly providing blockchain security policy and technology briefings to UNODC and Interpol, promoting the coordinated development of regulatory frameworks and technological capabilities. Leveraging its self-developed Skynet security platform and the industry's largest proprietary Web3 security database, CertiK has provided security services to 5,000 clients worldwide, continuously monitoring on-chain risk dynamics. As the digital asset market gradually becomes more institutionalized, the security system is evolving from a single technical service into a crucial infrastructure supporting regulatory compliance and market trust.

data.title: India's LPG Production Not Yet Self-Sufficient, Government Official Says
data.description: India's liquefied petroleum gas (LPG) production has not yet reached a level of self-sufficiency, according to a government official. According to Jin10, the official highlighted that the country still relies on imports to meet its domestic LPG demand. This statement underscores the ongoing challenges India faces in achieving energy independence, particularly in the LPG sector. The reliance on imports continues to be a significant factor in the country's energy strategy, as efforts to boost domestic production are ongoing. The official's comments reflect the broader context of India's energy policy, which aims to balance domestic production with import needs to ensure a stable energy supply.

data.title: U.S. Faces Potential Population Decline Impacting Workforce and Economy
data.description:
The United States is on the brink of experiencing a population decline for the first time in its 250-year history. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting concerns that this demographic shift could have significant repercussions for the nation's workforce and businesses. 

A shrinking population could lead to a reduced labor force, affecting productivity and economic growth. Experts warn that fewer workers may result in increased labor costs and a potential slowdown in innovation and competitiveness. 

The implications of a declining population extend beyond the economy, potentially impacting social services and infrastructure. Policymakers are urged to consider strategies to address these challenges, such as immigration reform and policies to encourage higher birth rates. 

As the U.S. grapples with these demographic changes, the focus will be on finding sustainable solutions to maintain economic stability and growth.

data.title: Kremlin Criticizes EU's Plan to Reduce Reliance on Russian LNG
data.description: The Kremlin has expressed disapproval of the European Union's initiative to reduce its dependence on Russian liquefied natural gas (LNG). According to Jin10, the Kremlin stated that Europeans are facing the consequences of their own actions. This response comes amid ongoing geopolitical tensions and efforts by the EU to diversify its energy sources away from Russia. The EU's strategy aims to enhance energy security and reduce vulnerability to potential supply disruptions. The Kremlin's remarks highlight the complex dynamics between Russia and the EU in the energy sector.
